diagrams.title: Lock Hierarchy Tree Structure
diagrams.description: Hierarchical tree showing different granularity levels from database (root) to field (leaves)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the structure on which multiple granularity locking operates, showing parent-child relationships between different data items.

diagrams.title: Lock Compatibility Matrix
diagrams.description: 6x6 matrix showing which lock modes can coexist
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for determining whether a lock request can be granted based on existing locks, essential for lock manager to make correct decisions.

diagrams.title: Lock Acquisition and Release Flow
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing the order of acquiring and releasing locks in MGL
elements: If parent has required lock, acquire child lock
Release locks in reverse order: leaf to root
Two phases: growing phase (acquire) and shrinking phase (release)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the protocol that transactions must follow to ensure correct locking without deadlocks and maintaining serializability.
